notes: Elicia's recording-booth roster. These are the people who walked into the booth tonight because the booth was the only place they could say it. Most are 'preoccupied' mood by default — they're not here for small talk. The mood ladder follows the Fool's pattern (preoccupied < talkative < lonely < intense).

visitors.name: The Returner
visitors.lore_text: Someone who recorded here once before. They remember the chair, the mic-stand height. They came back to listen to what they said the first time, before they say what they came to say tonight.

steps.greet: Nod. The returner nods back. The booth-chair fits them already.
steps.listen: They unfold the same story they recorded eight months ago. It is shorter this time. Smaller in a way that feels honest.
steps.deliver: You bring out the old tape. They hold it without playing it. Then they ask you to play it. Then they ask you to stop, halfway.
steps.sit_with: You sit at the console while they sit in the booth, and neither of you records. That counts as the session.

visitors.name: The Stranger in the Booth
visitors.lore_text: They didn't make an appointment. They were already sitting in the booth when you came in.

steps.greet: They look up. The look is heavier than the room is.
steps.listen: They start in the middle of a sentence that began before you were born.
steps.deliver: They ask for water. You bring water. They drink half. They cap the bottle for you.
steps.sit_with: You sit across the glass. They lean toward the mic. You lean toward the speaker. Five minutes pass. It's the right five minutes.

visitors.name: The Kid with the Letter
visitors.lore_text: A teenager carrying a letter they wrote to someone they will not send it to. They want the letter on tape instead. The booth, they think, is closer than the mailbox.

steps.greet: They are sixteen and trying very hard to look thirty.
steps.listen: They read the letter to you instead of into the mic first. You tell them that's fine.
steps.deliver: You hand them the foam pop-filter. They look at it like a sacred object.
steps.sit_with: You record the letter. They listen back to themselves through the headphones. They cry quietly. They thank you and leave. The tape is labelled in their handwriting before they go.

visitors.name: Son · Pre-Funeral
visitors.lore_text: His father isn't dead yet. He wants to record the eulogy now, because he's worried he won't have the words when the time comes.

steps.greet: He won't sit down. You let him stand.
steps.listen: He talks about the diner. He talks about a fishing trip. He talks about a thing his father did that was bad and a thing his father did that was good.
steps.deliver: You bring him a mug of water and the long-mic, the one for standing close.
steps.sit_with: You sit while he records the second take, which is shorter than the first take but better. He thanks you. He takes the tape with him.

visitors.name: The Writer
visitors.lore_text: Local writer working on a novel they've been working on for nine years. They come to the booth to read a chapter aloud to see if it works in a voice that isn't theirs.

steps.greet: They have a manuscript folder. The corners are rounded from carrying.
steps.listen: They read the chapter. Twice. The second reading is slower. It works the second time.
steps.deliver: You bring them coffee. Black. They drink half before they finish reading.
steps.sit_with: You sit and listen to the playback together. They mark the parts that landed. They take the tape AND a copy. They will not return for at least a month.

visitors.name: The Truth-Teller
visitors.lore_text: Someone here to say a true thing they have not said anywhere else. The booth is the only place small enough to hold it.

steps.greet: They will not look at you. They look at the mic.
steps.listen: They speak in a voice that has not been used at this volume in a long time.
steps.deliver: You hand them the headphones. They wave them off.
steps.sit_with: You sit at the console. You do not turn the playback on. You let the recording exist without being heard. That is what they came for.

visitors.name: The Apologist
visitors.lore_text: Coming to record an apology for someone they wronged who will not hear it directly. The tape is for themselves to be able to say it out loud, fully, once.

steps.greet: They thank you for being open this late.
steps.listen: They go through it: the name, the wrong, the consequence, the year, the silence, the regret.
steps.deliver: You bring tissues. They use one and then pocket the rest.
steps.sit_with: You sit through the playback. They wince. You don't react. They mark the tape DO NOT PLAY UNTIL I AM GONE. They leave it.

visitors.name: Elicia (off-shift)
visitors.lore_text: When the player is NOT Elicia, she comes in off-shift to listen at the console. When the player IS Elicia, this entry swaps out.

steps.greet: She nods from the doorway. Says she'll be at the console.
steps.listen: She tells you which tape from the library to put on, second drawer down.
steps.deliver: You bring her coffee. She remembers how you take yours.
steps.sit_with: She listens beside you while the next visitor records. She doesn't speak. The room is steadier with her in it.

visitors.name: Frasier (visiting)
visitors.lore_text: When the player is NOT Frasier, he comes over from the cathedral to sit in the lounge for a while. When the player IS Frasier, this entry swaps out.

steps.greet: He's holding a small balsa-wood object he didn't finish.
steps.listen: He talks about what he's making, not at length, not for long.
steps.deliver: You bring him water. He drinks it slowly.
steps.sit_with: You sit with him in the lounge. The mic isn't running. He says it helps to be somewhere that's listening even when nothing's being said.
